executiveIntent.problem: Axon is an ML-first systems language with first-class tensors, autograd, and LLVM codegen, but it lacks the automatic parallelism, operator fusion, and advanced compiler optimizations that define state-of-the-art ML compilers (XLA, TVM, Triton, JAX). Meanwhile, Bend — a massively parallel language built on interaction combinators — demonstrates that implicit parallelism, accumulative diagnostics, ordered transform passes, and linearity-aware compilation can be achieved in a Rust-based compiler with modest codebase size.
executiveIntent.goal: Integrate Bend's most impactful innovations into Axon's compiler pipeline to deliver: (1) automatic tensor-level parallelism without user annotations, (2) fused tensor operation kernels that eliminate memory-bandwidth bottlenecks, (3) an accumulative diagnostic system with per-warning severity control, (4) a modular transform-pass architecture that enables rapid optimization development, and (5) linearity annotations for guaranteed zero-copy tensor forwarding — making Axon the first ML language that combines PyTorch-level ergonomics with XLA-level compilation and Rust-level safety, all from a single unified source.
executiveIntent.sotaPositioning: No existing language achieves all five simultaneously: — PyTorch: ergonomic but interpreted, no compile-time safety — JAX/XLA: excellent fusion but Python-hosted, no ownership model — Triton: GPU-only, no general-purpose language features — Mojo: systems-level but closed-source, no tensor shape checking — Bend: implicit parallelism but 24-bit only, no tensors, no mutation — Axon (post-integration): all five pillars in one compiled language.

pillar.name: Automatic Tensor Parallelism
pillar.description: Data-dependency analysis at MIR level to identify and schedule independent tensor operations for concurrent execution across CPU threads and GPU streams, without user annotations.
pillar.bendInspiration: Bend's implicit parallelism via interaction net redex scheduling.
pillar.axonAdaptation: MIR-level DAG analysis of tensor operations, not interaction nets. CPU: thread pool dispatch. GPU: CUDA stream scheduling.
pillar.sotaBenchmark: Match or exceed JAX's auto-vectorization on standard ML benchmarks (MNIST, ResNet-18, Transformer-base).

pillar.name: Tensor Operation Fusion
pillar.description: Compile-time detection and fusion of tensor operation chains into single optimized kernels, eliminating intermediate tensor materialization and reducing memory bandwidth pressure by 5-10x.
pillar.bendInspiration: Bend's fusing functions and combinator floating — closed subexpression extraction to top-level definitions.
pillar.axonAdaptation: MIR pattern matching for common fusion patterns (linear+relu, matmul+bias+activation, reduction chains). Generate fused C runtime kernels or LLVM IR.
pillar.sotaBenchmark: Achieve within 2x of XLA's fused kernel throughput on element-wise chains and matmul+activation patterns.

pillar.name: Compiler Infrastructure Modernization
pillar.description: Accumulative diagnostics, modular transform passes, global name interning, stack-safe recursion, snapshot testing, and granular optimization flags — making Axon's compiler as maintainable as it is powerful.
pillar.bendInspiration: Bend's Diagnostics system (accumulative, configurable severity), 31-pass transform pipeline, stacker-based recursion, insta snapshot testing.
pillar.axonAdaptation: New src/diagnostics.rs module, src/mir/transform/ pass directory, global NameInterner, stacker integration, insta test infrastructure.
pillar.sotaBenchmark: Zero regressions during integration. All 1,034+ tests pass after every phase. Error message quality verified by snapshot tests.

pillar.name: Type System Extensions
pillar.description: Linearity annotations for zero-copy tensor forwarding, GPU-optimized ADT encoding strategies, and enhanced pattern matching with fold/reduce as first-class parallel primitives.
pillar.bendInspiration: Bend's affine variable semantics (auto-dup), Scott/NumScott ADT encoding, fold/bend loop constructs.
pillar.axonAdaptation: Optional linear/unique annotations on function parameters, configurable enum layouts for GPU targets, tensor-aware fold/reduce syntax sugar.
pillar.sotaBenchmark: Demonstrate zero-copy tensor forwarding in training loops. Measure allocation reduction vs non-linear baseline.

pillar.name: Language Ergonomics
pillar.description: Tensor comprehension syntax, monadic with-blocks for effectful pipelines, math-notation blocks, and list comprehensions — bringing Python-level expressiveness to a compiled, safe language.
pillar.bendInspiration: Bend's list comprehensions, with/ask monadic blocks, dual syntax system.
pillar.axonAdaptation: Tensor comprehension as syntactic sugar over iterators + tensor constructors. with-blocks for GPU pipeline staging. Math-notation as future exploration.
pillar.sotaBenchmark: Migration survey: Python ML developers can transliterate 80%+ of PyTorch idioms to Axon without consulting documentation.

framework.name: Hybrid-CO-STAR-RISEN-TIDD-EC
framework.why: Combines strategic clarity, procedural rigor, and strict do/don't boundaries for high-reliability compiler engineering by AI agents.

requiredSections.section: Project state, codebase location, relevant files, current test count.
Specific compiler engineering role for this task.
Single measurable outcome with acceptance criteria.
Ordered substeps with file paths and expected changes.
Required proof of completion (test output, diff, snapshot).
Expected output structure (files modified, tests added, summary).

framework.name: Chain-of-Density-Refinement
framework.why: Each execution iteration adds missing specificity while keeping constraints explicit and stable. Prevents prompt drift across long compiler engineering sessions.
framework.policy: Handoff packets between phases must be denser than the previous phase's packet — more facts, fewer words.

contextManagementStrategy.principle: Context is a controlled resource, not an unbounded transcript. Every token must earn its place.

controls.control: Phase handoff packets: decisions made, files changed, tests added, open risks, next actions. Max 2,000 tokens.
Rolling summaries with evidence: file paths, line numbers, test names. No prose narratives.
Pin non-negotiables in every prompt: test baseline (1,034+), zero-warning compilation, snapshot verification.
Token budget per phase: max 80K input tokens. Compact context before starting new phase.
Use subagents for exploration and heavy file reading. Keep main context for decisions and code changes.
Never read the entire codebase. Use targeted Grep/Glob for specific locations.

successMetrics.metric: All existing tests pass after every phase. No regressions.
At least 200 new tests covering all integrated features.
Fused tensor operations achieve at least 3x throughput vs unfused on element-wise chains.
Auto-parallelized independent tensor ops scale linearly on multi-core CPU.
Every compiler error has a structured code, source span, and suggestion.
New passes increase compile time by no more than 15% on the standard benchmark suite.
At least 5 common tensor patterns verified zero-copy via linearity analysis.
At least 100 golden-test snapshots covering parse, typecheck, MIR, LLVM IR, and error output.

phase.name: Infrastructure Foundation
phase.scope: Diagnostics system, MIR transform pass architecture, snapshot testing, stacker, name interning, CLI flags.
phase.gate: All 1,034 tests pass. Diagnostics accumulate across phases. At least 30 snapshot tests exist.

phase.name: Core Tensor Optimizations
phase.scope: Operator fusion, auto-parallelism, fold/reduce primitives, combinator floating for tensor kernels.
phase.gate: Fusion benchmarks hit 3x target. Parallelism scales on 4 cores. 100+ new tests.

phase.name: Type System Extensions
phase.scope: Linear/affine annotations, GPU-optimized ADT layouts, tensor comprehensions.
phase.gate: Zero-copy verified on 5+ patterns. ADT layout benchmarks complete.

phase.name: Language Ergonomics
phase.gate: New syntax fully documented. Migration guide updated. All tests pass.

phase.name: Advanced and GPU-Deferred
phase.scope: Math-notation blocks, GPU warp optimization, shape specialization. Requires MLIR pipeline.
phase.gate: Design documents complete. Implementation deferred to GPU codegen phase.
